channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A strike on a mosque kills 19 as Israel bombards northern Gaza and southern Beirut
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a mosque in the Gaza Strip early Sunday killed at least 19 people, Palestinian officials said, as Israel intensified its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Beirut in a widening war with Iran-allied militant groups across the region.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope names 21 new cardinals, significantly increasing the pool who will one day elect his successor
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis named 21 new cardinals Sunday, significantly increasing the size of the College of Cardinals and further cementing his mark on the group of prelates who will one day elect his successor.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tunisians vote in an election with one of President Saied's 2 challengers in prison
item.description: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — Tunisians cast ballots Sunday in the country’s third presidential election since the pro-democracy revolt known as the Arab Spring, while one of the candidates remains in prison.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 1 dead as Russia strikes Ukraine with drones and missiles
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — One person has died after Russian forces attacked Ukraine overnight with 87 Shahed drones and four different types of missiles, officials said Sunday. A 49-year-old man was killed in the Kharkiv region after his car was hit by a drone, said regional Gov. Oleh Syniehubov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: France's Macron calls for end to arms deliveries for use in Gaza
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron on Saturday called for a halt in deliveries of arms used in Gaza to Israel, adding that France was not sending arms to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Israel expands its bombardment in Lebanon as thousands flee widening war
item.description: MASNAA BORDER CROSSING, Lebanon (AP) — Powerful new explosions rocked Beirut's southern suburbs late Saturday as Israel expanded its bombardment in Lebanon, also striking a Palestinian refugee camp deep in the north for the first time as it targeted both Hezbollah and Hamas fighters.
item.dc:creator: BASSAM HATOUM, MELANIE LIDMAN and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Netanyahu calls Macron, other Western leaders who support arms embargo against Israel a ‘disgrace’
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu slammed Western leaders on Saturday who said they support an arms embargo on Israel over how its handling the Gaza war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Clinton Defense chief says Netanyahu 'does not have any confidence' with Biden
item.description: William Cohen, President Clinton’s former Secretary of Defense, hypothesized that Israel Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has “no confidence” in his relationship with President Biden in a Saturday Fox News appearance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Ex-NATO chief says allies should have given Ukraine weapons earlier
item.description: Former NATO chief Jens Stoltenberg shared in a new interview that one of his regrets was not ensuring Ukraine was stocked with weapons ahead of Russia’s invasion nearly three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Most Americans say Israel-Hamas war will lead to wider war: Survey
item.description: Most Americans said the Israel-Hamas war will lead to wider conflict in the region involving other countries, according to a survey released on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Former Defense chief: Israeli attack on Iran nuclear facilities would be 'significant strike'
item.description: Former Defense Secretary Mark Esper weighed in on Israel's likely response to a barrage of missiles launched by Tehran earlier this week and potential attacks on Iran nuclear facilities, saying it would be a "significant strike.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine downs a Russian warplane and Russia claims gains in the east
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian forces said they shot down a Russian fighter plane on Saturday while Russia claimed it made gains in Ukraine’s east.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Expert warns UN's role in AI regulation could lead to safety overreach
item.description: Nations have pursued a number of options to better globally coordinate artificial intelligence safety initiatives, including several safety summits and the creation of safety institutes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boris Johnson alleges Netanyahu may have bugged his bathroom
item.description: Israel also was accused around the same time of planting listening devices in The White House, The Telegraph also reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Sanxingdui Ruins were discovered accidentally by a farmer; has since been the site of over 60K relics
item.description: Sanxingdui Ruins is an archaeological site in Guanghan City. Official archaeological excavations have been conducted since 1934 at the site after its discovery in 1929.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two London police officers reinstated over stop and search of Black athletes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Two former London police officers have been handed their jobs back and will receive back pay after winning an appeal Friday against a ruling that said they had lied about smelling cannabis during a stop and search of two Black athletes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: An Israeli airstrike cuts a major highway linking Lebanon with Syria
item.description: An Israeli airstrike has cut off a main highway linking Lebanon with Syria, leaving two huge craters on either side of the road.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Wildlife lover facing charges after allegedly assaulting passersby while attempting to capture a baby seagull
item.description: A Belfast woman was in court Tuesday after allegedly attacking passersby who tried to intervene with her capture of a baby seagull in August.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tunisia votes Sunday in its third presidential election since the Arab Spring
item.description: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — With his major opponents imprisoned or left off the ballot, Tunisian President Kais Saied faces few obstacles to winning reelection on Sunday, five years after riding anti-establishment backlash to a first term. The North African country's Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK gives sovereignty of the Chagos Islands to Mauritius. The US base on Diego Garcia stays
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government agreed Thursday to hand sovereignty of the long-contested Chagos Islands, an archipelago of more than 60 islands in the Indian Ocean, to Mauritius, in a deal to secure the future of a strategically important U.K.-U.S. military base.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: G20 environment ministers back funding for forest conservation
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Environment ministers of the Group of 20 nations agreed Thursday to support the creation of funding sources for ecosystem services, acknowledging Brazil's proposal to establish a trust fund for forest conservation.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New NATO chief Mark Rutte visits Ukraine in his first trip since taking office
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — New NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte visited Ukraine on Thursday in his first official trip since taking office and pledging the alliance's continued support for Kyiv in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran warns of 'decisive response' if Israel crosses 'red lines'
item.description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian warned this week at the Asia Cooperation Dialogue summit that Israel cannot act with "impunity" and "crossing [Iran's] red lines" will be met with a "decisive response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike in Beirut killed 7 health and rescue workers
item.description: An Israeli strike overnight in the Lebanese capital Beirut killed seven health and rescue workers, an Islamic health organization said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel presses forward on two fronts and reports 8 combat deaths as fears of a wider war mount
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel pressed forward on two fronts Wednesday, pursuing a ground incursion into Lebanon against Hezbollah that left eight Israeli soldiers dead and conducting strikes in Gaza that killed dozens, including children.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: World War II-era bomb explodes at busy international airport, cratering taxiway
item.description: An American bomb from World War II buried beneath a Japanese airport exploded Wednesday, cratering a taxiway and forcing the cancellation of dozens of flights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese Communist Party marks 75 years of rule as economic, security threats persist
item.description: China is marking the 75th year of Communist Party rule. The state-controlled media reported on China's progress and stability, with no mention of the many challenges facing the nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: US destroyer fires ballistic missile interceptors to defend Israel against Iranian barrage
item.description: The USS Cole and USS Bulkeley fired about a dozen ballistic missile interceptors to defend Israel from a barrage of nearly 200 Iranian missiles on Tuesday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel bans UN secretary-general over anti-Israel actions: 'Doesn't deserve to set foot on Israeli soil'
item.description: The United Nations has increasingly faced criticism over the past year for how officials have responded to and handled the conflicts in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel, Iran swap threats as strikes on Lebanon continue
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has vowed to retaliate, saying Iran “made a big mistake" by launching a missile attack.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as 1st female president of Mexico
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in Tuesday as Mexico’s first female president, riding the enthusiasm over her predecessor’s social programs but also facing challenges that include stubbornly high levels of violence.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ and MARIANA MARTÍNEZ BARBA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What Iran attacks mean for Americans in Israel: Vittert
item.description: NewsNation's Leland Vittert believes there will not be immediate panic for most of the more than 500,000 Americans living in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Iran missile barrage on Israel 'appears to have been defeated'
item.description: Israel telegraphed that an attack of this sort could result in retaliation against significant Iranian targets.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: WikiLeaks' Julian Assange says he pleaded 'guilty to journalism' in order to be freed
item.description: STRASBOURG, France (AP) — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange said Tuesday that he was freed after years of incarceration because he "pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL BASTIEN, BARBARA SURK and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Who were the 7 high-ranking Hezbollah officials killed over the past week?
item.description: The move left Lebanon and much of the Mideast in shock as Israeli officials celebrated major military breakthroughs.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 148 are dead in floods and landslides in Nepal
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal (AP) — Rescuers in Nepal recovered dozens of bodies from buses and other vehicles that were buried in landslides near the capital Kathmandu, as the death toll from flooding rose to at least 148 with dozens missing, officials said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BINAJ GURUBACHARYA, Associated Press, NewsNation
